examples.title: Lambertian Reflection Model
examples.code:
ALGORITHM LambertianReflection
INPUT: lightIntensity, surfaceNormal, lightDirection
OUTPUT: observedIntensity

BEGIN
  observedIntensity = kₐIₐ + kₑIₑmax(0, n·l)
END
examples.explanation: Calculates observed intensity based on light intensity, surface normal, and light direction

examples.title: Specular Reflection Model
examples.code:
import math

def specular_reflection(light_intensity, surface_normal, light_direction, viewing_direction, shininess):
  dot_product = math.dot(surface_normal, light_direction)
  reflection_direction = 2 * dot_product * surface_normal - light_direction
  dot_product = math.dot(reflection_direction, viewing_direction)
  observed_intensity = kₛIₑmax(0, dot_product^shininess)
  return observed_intensity
examples.explanation: Calculates observed intensity based on light intensity, surface normal, light direction, viewing direction, and shininess

examples.title: Image Irradiance Equation
examples.explanation: Calculates image irradiance based on scene radiance, lens diameter, focal length, and theta
